title: hieracium levicaule subsp. parvuliceps
intro: Hieracium levicaule subsp. parvuliceps, also known as the small-headed hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The plant's medicinal properties are said to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Hieracium levicaule subsp. parvuliceps typically involve drying the plant's leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create a medicinal tea or tincture.
